footer.tagline: 3-in-1 solution for building frame and envelope.
footer.sub: Pre-engineered steel · High-performance insulation · Comprehensive waterproofing
footer.slogan: Honest. Clear. With you all the way.

home.hero_headline: A good construction doesn't start with many choices.
home.guided_lead: Perhaps you're thinking about a project from different angles.
home.guided_q: What do you want to be clearer about right now?
home.guided_c2: An idea that needs to stay true
home.guided_c3: A project that needs clear control
home.bridge: A lasting structure is not decided at its most visible part, but at the intersections that are easily overlooked.
home.bridge_sub: FrameX proactively integrates them from the start — so every detail connects coherently, and the entire structure operates as one complete whole.
home.pain_headline: Some projects aren't tired because they're complex. They're tired because they're split among too many parties.
home.pain_sub: The real pain is not in the drawings. It's in the disconnection.
home.pain_body: Homeowners worry about delays, constant change orders, and not knowing who to trust. A new build leaks after a few rainy seasons and repeated repairs don't resolve it. Architects are exhausted when design intent is broken on site. Investors face pressure on handover schedules, quality, and post-occupancy warranty. The common thread: structure, envelope and waterproofing are typically handled separately, leaving risks at the intersections.
home.pain_1_desc: Constant change orders, not knowing who to trust.
home.pain_2_desc: A new build needs repeated repairs after just a few rainy seasons.
home.pain_3_desc: A beautiful design is no longer intact when it reaches the site.
home.pain_4_desc: Schedule, quality and warranty weigh on the entire project.
home.pain_closing: Intersections are where risk usually begins.
home.solution_body: FrameX was formed with one clear goal: to create a complete construction solution where pre-engineered steel, high-performance insulation and comprehensive waterproofing are integrated from the start. Not to do more, but to reduce the gaps between parties and bring the project under one unified logic.
home.pillar_1_desc: Creates a synchronized foundation for schedule, coordination and execution.
home.pillar_2_desc: Keeps the building cooler, more stable, and better suited for a hot-humid climate.
home.pillar_3_desc: Reduces risk at intersection points that are easily overlooked or passed off between parties.
home.solution_signature: 1 synchronized drawing set. 1 continuous schedule. 1 accountable point of contact.
home.audience_headline: You may be standing at one of these three perspectives. Or all three at once.
home.audience_sub: Whether you think of the project as a place to live, an idea that needs to stay true, or a project that needs clear control — the starting point should always be integration from the start.
home.audience_1_copy: Build faster. Reduce ambiguous extras. Less worry about leaks. Cooler to live in comfortably. And most importantly, have one contact handle it instead of managing multiple parties yourself.
home.audience_2_copy: Keep concept to site. Coordinate details at intersections. Synchronize shopdrawings. Reduce conflicts with MEP and façade. Have a technical partner who understands their role is to protect the idea throughout execution.
home.audience_3_copy: One contact for frame and envelope. Clearer on schedule, quality, traceable records and warranty. Every change confirmed before construction to reduce risk and disputes over accountability.
home.projects_headline: A few structures that have taken shape
home.projects_body: FrameX doesn't just talk about a way of working. That approach has gone through many different contexts: from townhouses in narrow alleys, villas, coffee shops, homestays to commercial projects.
home.trust_headline: Trust doesn't come from promises. It comes from how a project is controlled.
home.trust_body: FrameX doesn't need to publish the full RACI, form pack, tracker or QC template. But behind the minimal experience is a real operating system: clear accountability, clear controls, clear handover standards and a clear process for handling changes before construction.
home.trust_1_desc: Controlled by checklist and ITP at important items.
home.trust_2_desc: Inspection stops at positions that shouldn't be rushed.
home.trust_3_desc: Water-test before handover where applicable, to detect risks earlier.
home.trust_4_desc: Every change is confirmed before construction; cost and schedule impacts are clarified.
home.trust_5_desc: Clients don't need to piece together multiple work streams and resolve the gaps themselves.
home.trust_6_desc: Clearer for operations, handover and warranty.
home.trust_closing: Honest. Clear. With you all the way.
home.final_headline: You can start from a floor plan, concept, RFQ or just a first question.
home.final_sub: FrameX will help you see the frame and envelope more clearly before everything becomes complicated. Whether you're thinking as a homeowner, architect or investor, the starting point should be clarity.
home.final_microcopy: Pricing follows actual scope / BOQ — not intuition or verbal agreement.

solution.headline: 3-in-1, so your project stays intact at its most critical intersections.
solution.sub: FrameX integrates pre-engineered steel, high-performance insulation and comprehensive waterproofing from the start so every detail connects more coherently.
solution.signature: 1 synchronized drawing set. 1 continuous schedule. 1 accountable point of contact.
solution.why_headline: Many projects don't face problems because of a lack of solutions. They face problems because the solution is split among too many parties.
solution.why_desc: When structure, envelope and waterproofing follow separate, disconnected workflows, risk doesn't sit in the obvious places — it sits at the intersections that are easily overlooked. That's when schedules stretch, concept gets broken, extras become opaque, and operational failures appear after handover.
solution.why_1_desc: Each part handled by a separate party, but no one truly owns the gap between them.
solution.why_2_desc: Drawings, details and site can diverge at the most critical positions.
solution.why_3_desc: Problems only surface during construction, handover or after occupancy.
solution.pillars_headline: Three different parts, but they need to be seen as one system
solution.pillar_1_desc: Creates a foundation for schedule, installation and alignment between design and execution.
solution.pillar_1_outcome: Faster to start on the right rhythm.
solution.pillar_2_desc: Makes the building more thermally stable, better suited for a hot-humid climate and improved occupant experience.
solution.pillar_2_outcome: Cooler to live and operate in comfortably.
solution.pillar_3_desc: Reduces risk at intersection points that are easily overlooked or passed off between parties.
solution.pillar_3_outcome: More durable so the building doesn't need repeated repairs.
solution.intersections_headline: Intersections are where the project needs to be thought through most carefully
solution.intersections_body: A lasting structure is not decided at its most visible part, but at the intersections that are easily overlooked. That's why FrameX doesn't just supply individual components — it proactively integrates them from the start so every detail connects coherently and the whole structure operates as one complete whole.
solution.package_headline: Each project needs a different level of completion
solution.package_intro: FrameX builds solutions at different levels suited to the context, needs and expectations of each project, rather than applying the same formula to every situation.
solution.pkg_1_fit: Best when the priority is execution efficiency and clarity of scope.
solution.pkg_2_fit: Best when the project demands a better experience in space, performance and finish.
solution.pkg_3_desc: Designed for the specific problem of each project.
solution.pkg_3_fit: Best when site conditions, terrain, function or concept require a distinct solution.
solution.pkg_microcopy: Pricing follows actual scope and BOQ — not a fixed price per m² applied to every situation.
solution.outcome_headline: Good results don't come from doing more. They come from connecting better.
solution.final_headline: If you have a floor plan, concept or RFQ, start from the intersections first.
solution.final_sub: FrameX can help you see the frame, envelope and highest-risk positions more clearly so the project can be executed coherently from the start.

investor.headline: A good project is not just delivered on time. It must run smoothly after that.
investor.sub: FrameX integrates frame, envelope and waterproofing from the start so schedule, quality and warranty aren't broken across multiple parties.
investor.pain_headline: When the intersections are split, risk doesn't disappear. It just moves to handover and warranty.
investor.pain_intro: For investors, pressure isn't only about breaking ground or hitting completion milestones. The real pressure is ensuring the project can be handed over, operated, traced back and kept free from disputes afterward.
investor.pain_1_desc: One slow link and the entire handover plan is affected.
investor.pain_2_desc: Separate frame, envelope and waterproofing makes it hard to assign blame for intersection failures.
investor.pain_3_desc: When changes aren't confirmed upfront, cost and timeline easily drift.
investor.pain_4_desc: Issues don't stop at construction — they extend into the operational phase.
investor.change_headline: FrameX brings the parts that easily break apart under one execution logic.
investor.change_body: Instead of letting structure, insulation and waterproofing follow separate, disconnected coordination tracks, FrameX integrates them from the start to reduce the gap between design, construction, handover and inspection.
investor.value_1_desc: Reduces overlap and shortens communication loops.
investor.value_2_desc: Lets all related items be seen within the same timeline.
investor.value_3_desc: Limits each party interpreting intersections differently.
investor.model_headline: A way of working that investors can control
investor.model_1_desc: Pricing follows actual scope and BOQ — not intuitive estimates.
investor.model_2_desc: Checklist-based control with hold points at positions that shouldn't be rushed.
investor.model_3_desc: Every change confirmed upfront, with cost and schedule impact made clear.
investor.model_4_desc: Helps handover, operations and warranty stay informed.
investor.proof_headline: A few projects showing this logic working in the real world
investor.trust_headline: Trust comes from how the project is controlled
investor.trust_3: Stop Work when safety isn't assured
investor.final_headline: If you're preparing an RFQ, timeline or execution scope, FrameX can start alongside you from there.
investor.final_sub: No need to start with everything at once. Just enough data to look clearly at the frame, envelope and intersections of the project together.
investor.cta_microcopy: Response based on the actual scope of your project.

contractor.headline: A cleaner site starts with clearer boundaries.
contractor.sub: FrameX participates as a frame & envelope partner so the intersections between drawings, site and construction responsibility don't become gaps.
contractor.pain_headline: Contractors aren't afraid of hard work. They're afraid of overlapping parts that no one has clearly settled.
contractor.pain_intro: When the schedule is already tight, just one unresolved intersection point and the site starts losing rhythm. Not just delays — but disputes about scope, details, sequencing and handover.
contractor.pain_1_desc: Gaps or overlaps in work scope are only discovered once on site.
contractor.pain_2_desc: Each party interprets differently, leaving execution details inconsistent.
contractor.pain_3_desc: Technical layers clash at the most important positions.
contractor.pain_4_desc: Without a CO before construction, the site easily gets confused.
contractor.fit_headline: FrameX doesn't make the construction process heavier. FrameX clarifies the part most likely to cause friction.
contractor.fit_body: FrameX's value lies in integrating structure, insulation and waterproofing from the start, so intersection points are resolved within one logical system rather than waiting until site to untangle them.
contractor.fit_1_desc: Who does what, to what extent, and what they hand over — clarified early.
contractor.fit_2_desc: Helps execution stay closer to technical intent and real site conditions.
contractor.fit_3_desc: Especially at positions likely to clash with MEP, façade and finishes.
contractor.flow_headline: A lean coordination rhythm so the site isn't disrupted
contractor.flow_2_desc: Identify what needs to be clarified before execution.
contractor.flow_4_desc: Coordinate with all parties within the same timeline.
contractor.flow_5_desc: No changes going into construction by word of mouth.
contractor.proof_headline: A few contexts where getting the coordination right from the start made a difference
contractor.trust_headline: Good coordination starts from what shouldn't be left ambiguous
contractor.final_headline: If you have drawings, a BOQ or a timeline that needs reviewing, start from the intersections first.
contractor.final_sub: FrameX can help you look back at the points most likely to cause friction so the site runs more smoothly.
contractor.cta_microcopy: Response based on actual documents and site context.

architect.headline: A good idea shouldn't be lost at the intersections.
architect.sub: FrameX works alongside you as a technical partner to keep concept to site, synchronize shopdrawings and reduce conflicts at the positions most likely to go wrong.
architect.pain_headline: The gap between idea and site usually isn't in the concept. It's in how the details get resolved.
architect.pain_intro: Many designs aren't lost because the idea is weak. They're broken at the intersections between structure, envelope, waterproofing, MEP and real-world execution.
architect.pain_1_desc: The overall idea is no longer intact when it reaches the field.
architect.pain_2_desc: The most important positions are the ones most easily misread.
architect.pain_3_desc: Execution documents don't preserve the spirit of the design.
architect.pain_4_desc: MEP, façade and finishes pull each other away from the original concept.
architect.protect_headline: FrameX doesn't replace architecture. FrameX helps ideas make it to the site.
architect.protect_body: By integrating structure, insulation and waterproofing from the start, FrameX handles the parts that typically break down between design and construction — so concept isn't sacrificed by patchwork decisions made later.
architect.protect_1_title: Keep concept at the detail level
architect.protect_1_desc: Not just the overall image — but the operational logic of the design.
architect.protect_2_desc: So the positions most likely to distort design aren't left to the site to figure out.
architect.protect_3_desc: Keeps execution documents closer to the design intent.
architect.protect_4_desc: Especially at positions where multiple work layers converge.
architect.collab_headline: A coordination rhythm that's thorough without making the design process heavier
architect.collab_1_desc: From sketch to developed drawings — either can be the starting point.
architect.collab_2_desc: Identify what needs early resolution to protect the idea.
architect.collab_3_desc: Without breaking concept, without pushing risk to the site.
architect.collab_4_desc: Keep construction documents and site reality aligned with the design.
architect.proof_headline: A few projects where keeping the idea true made a difference
architect.trust_headline: Respect for design through clarity in technical execution
architect.final_headline: If you have a concept, documents or a detail you're not confident about, start from there.
architect.final_sub: FrameX can help you review the positions most likely to go wrong so the idea isn't lost during execution.
architect.cta_microcopy: Response based on actual documents and the technical requirements of the project.

projects.headline: Structures built through a clearer way of working.
projects.sub: Each project is a different context. But the common thread is how FrameX integrates frame, envelope and waterproofing so the build runs more coherently from the start.
projects.filter_headline: Find by building type or context closest to your project
projects.grid_headline: Not every project is the same. But the parts that need to be clarified always are.
projects.grid_body: In every different context, FrameX focuses on one thing: looking earlier at the intersections to reduce risks in construction, handover and operations.
projects.case_headline: Each case isn't just to look beautiful. Each case should help you make better decisions.
projects.final_headline: If you have a project with many overlapping parts, start with a case close to your context.
projects.final_sub: Or send a floor plan, timeline, concept or RFQ so FrameX can help you see the intersections clearly before everything gets complicated.

blog.headline: Worth reading before a project gets complicated
blog.sub: Not company news. This is where FrameX writes about intersections, risks often overlooked, and approaches that make projects clearer from the start.
blog.pillars_headline: Four content groups, all pointing to one thing: understand correctly before moving fast
blog.pillar_1_desc: Articles that start from the real worries of clients.
blog.pillar_2_desc: Comparing materials, solutions and fit by context.
blog.pillar_3_desc: Breaking down real projects to extract what's worth learning.
blog.pillar_4_desc: Explaining processes and standards that make projects more durable.
blog.why_headline: FrameX doesn't write to fill a News section. FrameX writes to help clients make better decisions.
blog.why_body: A good article doesn't need to be frequent. It just needs enough depth for the reader to understand the problem they're facing more accurately.
blog.final_headline: If you have a question about a project, it's likely also where an article should begin.
blog.final_sub: You can keep reading, send a question, or send a floor plan / documents so FrameX can look at it with you from a more practical angle.

contact.headline: Start from a floor plan, concept, RFQ or just a first question
contact.sub: Whether you're looking at the project as an investor, contractor or architect — the starting point should be clarity.
contact.name: Full name
contact.success: Thank you. We'll be in touch shortly.
contact.error: Something went wrong. Please try again.
contact.promise: Your documents are used solely for project evaluation purposes.

about.headline: FrameX
about.sub: 3-in-1 solution for building frame and envelope.
about.mission: We believe a good project begins with clarity — in commitment, in cost, and in every handover point. FrameX was built to be the single partner accountable for the entire frame and envelope.
